skill.name: scikit-bio
skill.description: A Python bioinformatics toolkit for sequence, phylogeny, and microbiome/community-ecology analysis; use it when you need to compute diversity/ordination/statistics from biological data and standard formats (FASTA/FASTQ/Newick/BIOM).
skill.summary: Bioinformatics toolkit for sequence, phylogeny, and microbiome analysis in Python using scikit-bio.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 118 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate scikit-bio bioinformatics toolkit wrapper. Markdown code block delimiters (```python) were misidentified as shell backtick execution. Variable names for principal coordinates (PC1, PC2) were misidentified as C2 keywords. No prompt injection attacks, malware indicators, or actual security threats were found. The skill contains only documentation files with Python code examples.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Backtick Execution
medium_findings.description: False positive: 98 instances of Markdown code block delimiters (```python) and documentation formatting in api_reference.md and SKILL.md were misidentified as Ruby/shell backtick commands. These are legitimate Python code examples showing scikit-bio API usage for sequence analysis, phylogenetics, and diversity calculations.

low_findings.title: C2 Keywords False Positive
low_findings.description: False positive: 'PC2' refers to Principal Coordinate 2 (ordination axis), a standard bioinformatics term for principal component axes. Found in PCoA plotting code examples.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
low_findings.description: False positive: No cryptographic algorithms are present. The flagged 'DNA' sequences (ATCG characters) are biological nucleotide sequences, and 'TreeNode' operations are tree data structure manipulations. These are bioinformatics operations, not cryptography.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
low_findings.description: False positive: Performance caching advice in api_reference.md and audit metadata in scikit-bio_audit_result_v1.json. No system reconnaissance activity detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL False Positive
low_findings.description: False positive: Source attribution link to the GitHub repository at github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. Legitimate citation of the skill origin, not a malicious URL.

content.user_title: Analyze biological sequences and microbiome data with scikit-bio
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics analysis requires complex statistical methods and specialized data formats that are difficult to implement correctly. This skill provides direct access to scikit-bio proven implementations for sequence analysis, phylogenetics, and microbiome diversity calculations.
actual_capabilities: Parse, validate, and manipulate biological sequences (DNA, RNA, Protein) with alphabet-aware operations including reverse complement, transcription, and translation.
Compute alpha diversity (Shannon, Faith PD, Simpson) and beta diversity (Bray-Curtis, UniFrac) metrics from abundance data.
Build, transform, and compare phylogenetic trees using Newick I/O, Neighbor Joining, UPGMA, midpoint rooting, and tree pruning.
Perform ordination (PCoA, CCA) and statistical hypothesis tests (PERMANOVA, ANOSIM, Mantel) on distance matrices.
Read and write standard bioinformatics file formats including FASTA, FASTQ, Newick, and BIOM tables.
Generate pairwise and multiple sequence alignments with Smith-Waterman (SSW) support and scoring matrices.
limitations: Does not include machine learning models for metagenomic classification or taxonomic assignment.
Large-scale genomic analysis may require additional tools for assembly, variant calling, or read mapping.
Sequence alignment performance is limited by Python overhead; use dedicated aligners for whole-genome comparisons.
Visualization output requires separate matplotlib or seaborn integration; no built-in plotting commands.

use_cases.title: Microbiome diversity analysis for biomedical researchers
use_cases.description: A biomedical researcher can compute alpha and beta diversity metrics from 16S rRNA sequencing data, perform PCoA ordination, and run PERMANOVA to test for significant differences between patient groups.

use_cases.title: Phylogenetic tree reconstruction for evolutionary biologists
use_cases.description: An evolutionary biologist can build phylogenetic trees from distance matrices using Neighbor Joining or UPGMA, prune and reroot trees, and compute patristic distances between taxa.

use_cases.title: Sequence manipulation for bioinformatics students
use_cases.description: A student learning bioinformatics can parse FASTA files, manipulate DNA, RNA, and protein sequences, perform reverse complement and translation operations, and run pairwise alignments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic sequence analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this DNA sequence using scikit-bio: [sequence]. Compute its reverse complement, find all start codon (ATG) positions, and calculate k-mer frequencies for k=3.

prompt_templates.title: Microbiome diversity calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an OTU count table with [N] samples and [M] features. Use scikit-bio to compute Shannon alpha diversity for each sample and Bray-Curtis beta diversity between all sample pairs. Then perform PCoA ordination and run PERMANOVA with [N] groups.

prompt_templates.title: Phylogenetic tree construction from distance matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a phylogenetic tree from this distance matrix using scikit-bio Neighbor Joining implementation. The matrix has taxa labeled [labels]. Root the tree at the midpoint and print it in ASCII format.

prompt_templates.title: Custom bioinformatics workflow integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bioinformatics workflow that reads a BIOM table and a Newick tree file, computes weighted UniFrac distances between samples, performs PCoA ordination, and exports the results as a pandas DataFrame for downstream statistical analysis.

output_examples.input: Compute Shannon alpha diversity for 3 microbiome samples with counts [[10,0,3,1],[0,8,2,0],[5,1,0,4]]
output_examples.output: Sample S1: Shannon index = 1.03, Sample S2: Shannon index = 0.87, Sample S3: Shannon index = 1.16. Sample S3 has the highest species evenness while Sample S2 has the lowest.

output_examples.input: Find all motifs matching ACG followed by any 3 nucleotides in DNA sequence ATGCGATCGATGCATCG
output_examples.output: Two motif matches found at positions 1 and 9. Both match pattern ACG followed by T at position 1 and A at position 9.

output_examples.input: Build a neighbor-joining tree from a distance matrix for taxa A, B, C, D
output_examples.output: Tree constructed with 4 taxa. The NJ algorithm groups A and B (distance 5) and C and D (distance 8) as sister clades, suggesting a closer relationship between A and B.

best_practices: Always validate input data types and ranges before computation. scikit-bio expects non-negative integer counts for diversity metrics, not relative frequencies.
Use generators and file streaming for large sequence files instead of loading all data into memory at once.
Save intermediate results like distance matrices and ordination objects to avoid recomputing expensive calculations during exploratory analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not use relative abundance data for phylogenetic diversity metrics like Faith PD or UniFrac. These require actual integer count data.
Avoid modifying TreeNode objects in-place during traversal. Create copies before modifying subtrees to prevent unexpected side effects.
Do not ignore sequence validation errors. scikit-bio DNA, RNA, and Protein classes enforce alphabet constraints; bypassing them can produce invalid biological results.

faq.question: What is scikit-bio used for?
faq.answer: scikit-bio is a Python library for bioinformatics. It provides tools for sequence analysis, phylogenetic trees, diversity calculations, and statistical tests for microbiome data.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports FASTA, FASTQ, Newick, and BIOM formats. It can read and write these standard bioinformatics file formats.

faq.question: Do I need to install scikit-bio separately?
faq.answer: Yes. You need to install scikit-bio version 0.6.0 or later using pip install scikit-bio. The skill requires Python with numpy and pandas installed.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze large genomic datasets?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for small to medium-scale analyses. For whole-genome assembly or variant calling, use dedicated tools like BWA or GATK.

faq.question: Does this skill support visualization?
faq.answer: This skill performs calculations and data transformations. For visualization, integrate with matplotlib, seaborn, or other plotting libraries separately.

faq.question: What types of biological sequences are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports DNA, RNA, and Protein sequences with alphabet-aware validation. It handles IUPAC nucleotide codes, gap characters, and quality scores from FASTQ files.
